fact: Deferred CodeSift tool loaders depend on exact module paths and string-keyed exports, and some facade-to-registry dependencies are safe only because the facade is imported lazily.
recommendation: When splitting a deferred tool module, retain its original path as a thin facade, preserve every runtime export name, and avoid turning registry imports eager.

fact: Astro template parsing is consumed through the stable parseAstroTemplate facade while preprocessing, resolution, mutable state, scanning, and tag processing can remain private modules.
recommendation: Keep the original parser module as the public facade when splitting parser states, and test the facade plus downstream extractor/island consumers rather than importing private state modules.
